title: 移民英國 香港出糧

replies.msg:
我個情況係香港公司比我移民去英國 Remote咁做香港D野, 照出糧比我

想請教下
1. BNO個5+1係咪唔使係當地顧主請, 就算失業都好, 住夠5+1年就得?
2. 稅收問題. 我見英國香港有簽 "雙重課稅寬免". 但Google完同睇埋d law都唔係好肯定點計法. 我既理解係香港收完稅之後, 英國會再收一次, 但英國收既稅係減去香港比左出去既稅.
e.g. 假設英國收我5萬稅, 香港收我1萬稅, 咁由於係香港比左1萬蚊, 所以英國呢邊就交4萬蚊. 正確嗎?
問你公司會計

1. 住夠5+1年就得
咁好可以remote做野
你間公司真係好人到爆
樓主做邊行
做個良好公民，交足稅

樓主問的是
交左香港稅後，英國那邊的稅是否可扣

回樓主，問公司會計都未必識答
直接問英國的稅局

quote.msg: 英國唔係全球稅咩

replies.msg:
正確
英國全球稅, 只要係英國一年中有一半時間居住英國就全球邊到賺錢都要交稅
而香港行地區基準, 即係賺香港錢就要交香港稅, 不論你住邊到都好

所以會兩邊一齊收稅
但英國香港同時又行"雙重課稅寬免"

所以我唔係好知結果會係下面邊一種:
1. 英國收晒我D稅先, 然後我Claim香港話我係英國交左所以唔使交香港稅
2. 香港收左我d稅先, 然後用英國稅再計一次, 減去香港交左既稅, 交剩餘既數比英國
3. 其他計算方式

我都未移民, 我覺得我就咁問英國稅局佢應該會唔理我
所以希望會有d有經驗人士分享下

replies.msg:
https://www.gov.uk/tax-uk-income-live-abroad/taxed-twice#:~:text=You%20may%20not%20have%20to,after%20you've%20been%20taxed

You may not have to pay twice if the country you’re resident in has a ‘double-taxation agreement’ with the UK. Depending on the agreement, you can apply for either:

partial or full relief before you’ve been taxed
a refund after you’ve been taxed

所以香港出糧應該只係比香港稅

e.g. 假設英國收我5萬稅, 香港收我1萬稅, 咁由於係香港比左1萬蚊, 所以英國呢邊就交4萬蚊. 正確嗎?

replies.msg:
You will report your full income and pay taxes on income from HK.
If you paid any income tax to HK Inland Revenue, those tax can reduce you tax payable to England.

However, I am not sure if you have to pay any income tax to HK because you are no longer a resident of HK. Check this out with your HK tax adviser.





Tax on foreign income

Contents
Overview
UK residence and tax
'Non-domiciled' residents
Reporting your foreign income
Foreign income that's taxed differently
If you're taxed twice
If you come to study in the UK
Overview
You may need to pay UK Income Tax on your foreign income, such as:

wages if you work abroad
foreign investment income, for example dividends and savings interest
rental income on overseas property
income from pensions held overseas
Foreign income is anything from outside England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland. The Channel Islands and the Isle of Man are classed as foreign.

Working out if you need to pay
Whether you need to pay depends on if you’re classed as ‘resident’ in the UK for tax.

If you’re not UK resident, you will not have to pay UK tax on your foreign income.

If you’re UK resident, you’ll normally pay tax on your foreign income. But you may not have to if your permanent home (‘domicile’) is abroad.

Reporting foreign income
If you need to pay tax, you usually report your foreign income in a Self Assessment tax return. But there’s some foreign income that’s taxed differently.

If your income is taxed in more than one country
You may be able to claim tax relief if you’re taxed in more than one country.

If you’ve not yet paid tax on the foreign income, you may need to apply for a certificate of residence to prove you’re eligible for relief.

Tax on foreign income

Contents
Overview
UK residence and tax
'Non-domiciled' residents
Reporting your foreign income
Foreign income that's taxed differently
If you're taxed twice
If you come to study in the UK
Overview
You may need to pay UK Income Tax on your foreign income, such as:

wages if you work abroad
foreign investment income, for example dividends and savings interest
rental income on overseas property
income from pensions held overseas
Foreign income is anything from outside England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland. The Channel Islands and the Isle of Man are classed as foreign.

Working out if you need to pay
Whether you need to pay depends on if you’re classed as ‘resident’ in the UK for tax.

If you’re not UK resident, you will not have to pay UK tax on your foreign income.

If you’re UK resident, you’ll normally pay tax on your foreign income. But you may not have to if your permanent home (‘domicile’) is abroad.

Reporting foreign income
If you need to pay tax, you usually report your foreign income in a Self Assessment tax return. But there’s some foreign income that’s taxed differently.

If your income is taxed in more than one country
You may be able to claim tax relief if you’re taxed in more than one country.

If you’ve not yet paid tax on the foreign income, you may need to apply for a certificate of residence to prove you’re eligible for relief.

所以我唔係好知結果會係下面邊一種:
1. 英國收晒我D稅先, 然後我Claim香港話我係英國交左所以唔使交香港稅
2. 香港收左我d稅先, 然後用英國稅再計一次, 減去香港交左既稅, 交剩餘既數比英國
3. 其他計算方式

我都未移民, 我覺得我就咁問英國稅局佢應該會唔理我
所以希望會有d有經驗人士分享下

https://www.gov.uk/tax-uk-income-live-abroad/taxed-twice#:~:text=You%20may%20not%20have%20to,after%20you've%20been%20taxed

You may not have to pay twice if the country you’re resident in has a ‘double-taxation agreement’ with the UK. Depending on the agreement, you can apply for either:

partial or full relief before you’ve been taxed
a refund after you’ve been taxed

所以香港出糧應該只係比香港稅

所以香港出糧應該只係比香港稅
Wrong... Tax liabilities is based on your tax residency...
If you live in UK for more than 183 days within a calendar year, you have to pay UK income tax on all your world wide income..
